displayTitle: Wash to Background, Never Past It
kicker: PT LEVEL I // REMOVAL OF SURFACE PENETRANT

beats.narration: Removal is the razor's edge of penetrant testing: lift the excess off the surface without pulling the penetrant back out of the flaws.

payload.title: REMOVING THE EXCESS
payload.sub: Off the surface, never out of the flaws.

beats.narration: Two ways to fail. Underwash leaves background glow that buries fine indications. Overwash strips the flaw itself, and the part looks perfect inspecting clean.

chips.s: Strips the flaw — looks clean, reads false

beats.narration: Technique follows the method. A rinses with water. C is the dry-then-dampened solvent wipe. B and D apply a separate, timed emulsifier for high-sensitivity work.
Read the wash under U-V. Overwashed: dark and clean, but the indication is gone. Correct: crisp line on a dark field. Underwashed: haze hides everything.

payload.heading: Three wash endpoints under UV-A
payload.note: OVERWASH STRIPS · CORRECT REVEALS · UNDERWASH HIDES

beats.narration: An overwashed part looks beautiful, and that is the trap. So wash to background, then stop. Steady hands pass; hurried ones strip flaws.

payload.sub: A clean-looking part can be a stripped one.
